data.title: The total open interest of GIGGLE contracts on the entire network exceeded US$100 million, setting a new record high.
data.description: Coinglass data shows that the total holdings of GIGGLE contracts across the entire network have reached 515,000, and the holding scale has exceeded US$100 million. It is currently reported at US$110 million, setting a historical high and a 24-hour increase of 254.35%. Among them, Binance GIGGLE contract holdings are 303,900 (US$65.6161 million), with a 24-hour increase of 265.76%; Bybit's GIGGLE contract holdings are 60,500 (US$13.0661 million), with a 24-hour increase of 209.04%.

data.title: Central Banks to Hold Key Meetings Amid U.S. Government Shutdown
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the upcoming week will see several central banks, including the Federal Reserve, European Central Bank, and Bank of Japan, holding interest rate meetings. This comes as the U.S. government continues its shutdown, resulting in fewer significant data releases from the United States.

On Thursday at 02:00 UTC+8, the Federal Reserve will announce its interest rate decision, followed by a monetary policy press conference with Fed Chair Jerome Powell at 02:30 UTC+8. On Friday, Dallas Fed President Logan, a 2026 FOMC voting member, is scheduled to speak at 01:15 and again at 21:30 UTC+8.

Powell is expected to announce a 25 basis point rate cut but is unlikely to significantly alter his future policy stance until there is a clearer understanding of inflation and the labor market. Given recent trade tensions and the ongoing government shutdown, Powell and his colleagues may adopt a slightly dovish tone, though not excessively so. Even dovish members like Waller are hesitant to commit to a series of rate cuts in advance. If the Fed questions the market's expectation of a 100 basis point rate cut after October, traders may face increased disappointment.

data.title: First XRP ETF Tops $100 Million Amid SEC Delay on New Approvals
data.description:
XRP’s first US exchange-traded fund (ETF) has crossed a significant milestone. It reached more than $100 million in assets under management (AUM) barely a month after launch. On October 24, REX-Osprey confirmed that its XRPR product surpassed the mark, signaling strong institutional appetite for regulated exposure to the digital asset. XRP ETF AUM Surpasses $100 Million The fund, launched in September, offers direct spot access to XRP and has quickly attracted investors seeking compliant avenues to diversify their portfolios
source: https://beincrypto.com/rex-xrp-etf-100-million-first-month/

data.title: Decentralized AI inference network DGrid AI announced that it has secured seed round investment and will soon launch node pre-sales.
data.description: DGrid AI, a decentralized AI inference network, has officially announced that it has secured seed round investment from Waterdrip Capital, IoTeX, Paramita VC, and other institutions. DGrid, leveraging its core "AI RPC + LLM inference + distributed node" architecture, is committed to breaking the monopoly of centralized AI platforms and providing open, reliable, and low-cost AI capabilities for the Web3 ecosystem. The raised funds will be primarily used to build its node network and expand ecosystem partnerships. Furthermore, to build a truly community-driven decentralized network, DGrid has officially announced the upcoming launch of its node presale program. This program will allow community members to participate in the decentralized AI network's construction by operating nodes and share in its early growth. Specific rules and schedule will be announced through official channels. DGrid AI is a decentralized AI inference network that provides developers and applications with low-cost, verifiable, and trustless AI capabilities.

data.title: Bitcoin News: Bitcoin Miners Extend Rally After Jane Street Discloses Stakes in Bitfarms, Cipher Mining, and Hut 8
data.description: Shares of leading Bitcoin mining companies surged after Jane Street Group, one of the world’s largest trading firms, disclosed new holdings in multiple miners — extending a months-long rally in publicly traded crypto mining stocks.Key TakeawaysJane Street revealed passive stakes of roughly 5% each in Bitfarms, Cipher Mining, and Hut 8.The stocks surged between 8% and 19% following the disclosure.Other Bitcoin miners, including Hive Digital and American Bitcoin Corp., also saw double-digit gains.Bitcoin mining stocks have outperformed BTC in 2025, with some posting over 200% annual gains.Jane Street Discloses New Bitcoin Mining StakesRegulatory filings submitted to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on Thursday showed that Jane Street’s trading affiliates hold:5.4% of Bitfarms (BITF),5% of Cipher Mining (CIFR), and5% of Hut 8 (HUT).The firm described these as passive trading positions, rather than activist stakes — but the disclosure was enough to fuel investor optimism across the sector.Mining Stocks Rally on Institutional ConfidenceFollowing the news, crypto mining shares rallied sharply on Thursday and continued higher into Friday’s session.At Friday’s market close:Cipher Mining (CIFR) jumped 19.73%,Hut 8 (HUT) gained 17.27%, andBitfarms (BITF) climbed 10.68%, according to Yahoo Finance data.Other publicly traded miners also advanced:American Bitcoin Corp. rose 11.29%,IREN Limited gained 12.60%, andHive Digital Technologies surged 17.77%.Jane Street’s disclosure follows Google’s recent 5.4% stake acquisition in Cipher Mining on Sept. 25, underscoring growing institutional interest in the Bitcoin mining industry.Bitcoin Miners Outperform Bitcoin in 2025Bitcoin mining remains a core component of the cryptocurrency ecosystem, ensuring network security and transaction validation through computational work.However, the sector’s publicly traded companies have become standout performers this year.Over the past 12 months:Bitfarms has risen 131%,Hut 8 has soared 211%, andBitcoin (BTC) is up 73%, according to Yahoo Finance.The data highlights how Bitcoin mining equities have outpaced BTC itself, as investors increasingly view mining firms as leveraged plays on the broader crypto market.Industry OutlookWith institutional players like Jane Street and Google taking positions in mining companies, analysts expect continued momentum in the sector.Upcoming catalysts include energy efficiency upgrades, AI-computing integrations, and potential regulatory clarity in the U.S. energy and infrastructure sectors.
